per_trial.rationale: The hypothesis_statement is quantitative and bounded (|e| ≤ |v*|·Δt + ε_quant) and explicitly tied to the 10 Hz update_rate, satisfying the level-3 criterion. controlled_variables enumerates concrete IV levels for v* and T plus a thorough confound table (cmd_vel_timeout, ramp, GUI, mock-hardware flag, clock). recorded_signals lists topics with native rates and per-topic justification including /joint_states correspondence to read() cycles, and success_thresholds gives four numeric, unit-bearing thresholds derived from the hypothesis (slope test, per-cycle residual, null). The visualization_plan specifies four figures with signal-by-signal rationale, failure_modes enumerates six concrete modes each with mitigations, and repo_grounding cites specific lines in diffbot_system.cpp (192, 215, 150–152), the controllers yaml, the read→update→write order, and the isnan-only init quirk—well above generic ROS 2 boilerplate.

The hypothesis_statement is quantitative with an explicit error bound tied to the controller's Δt and even includes a secondary structural hypothesis. controlled_variables enumerates concrete IV levels with replicates plus a thorough confounds table (update rate, hardware plugin, cmd_vel_timeout, sim/wall clock, RViz). recorded_signals lists topics with native rates and per-topic rationale anchored to the read/write cycle. success_thresholds (S1–S4) are numeric, unit-bearing, and derived from the hypothesis (including slope/intercept regression and floating-point structural test). The visualization_plan specifies four figures with tools (rosbag2_py/pandas/matplotlib) and per-figure signal rationale. failure_modes lists six concrete failure modes each with specific mitigations. repo_grounding is exceptional — line numbers in diffbot_system.cpp, the read/write cycle ordering, the `isnan` activation guard, the cmd_vel_timeout from diffbot_controllers.yaml, and the TwistStamped migration are all leveraged to inform experimental decisions.

The plan excels on every rubric dimension. The hypothesis is quantitative with an explicit error bound |e| ≤ |v*|·Δt grounded in the 10 Hz update_rate, plus a stronger structural H2. Controlled variables include concrete velocity/duration levels with replicates and an extensive confounds table (cmd_vel_timeout, acceleration limits, mock-hardware flag, GUI, clock). Recorded signals are listed per-topic with native rates and specific rationale, and success thresholds (S1–S4) are unit-bearing and derived directly from the hypothesis. The visualization plan specifies four distinct figures with signal-by-signal rationale, six failure modes each have specific mitigations, and the repo grounding cites exact line numbers in diffbot_system.cpp (read/write Euler step, isnan activation guard), the yaml config values, and the controller_manager read→update→write ordering — clearly tied to this repo's runtime, not generic ROS 2.
